items.back: 1. Forward current flight plan/control info as flight progresses. 2. Coordinate in sufficient time for receiving facility analysis (timing may be in LOA). 3. Coordinate when protected airspace overlaps common boundary. 4. Forward estimate revisions of 3 minutes or more. 5. Coordinate IFR/VFR flights with adjacent facilities for service continuation.
Revisions of 3 minutes or more must be forwarded to the appropriate ATS facility.

items.front: 8-2-2: Six rules for transfer of control in oceanic ops
items.back: a. One ATC unit controls at a time. b. Transfer at estimated control boundary or agreed time/point. c. Transferring unit forwards changed flight plan/control data. d. Accepting unit notifies if unable to accept; specifies required conditions. e. Accepting unit must not alter clearance before transfer point without prior approval. f. Nonradar: communications transfer 5 min before boundary unless otherwise agreed.
5 minutes before the aircraft is estimated to reach the boundary, unless otherwise agreed by the control and/or communication units.

items.front: May the accepting unit alter an aircraft's clearance before the transfer point?
items.back: No. Per 8-2-2e, the accepting unit must not alter the clearance without the prior approval of the transferring unit.

items.front: 8-2-3: Two governing documents for AIDC coordination
items.back: 1. Regional Interface Control Documents. 2. Letters of agreement between the facilities concerned.

items.front: What is AIDC and what governs its use?
items.back: Air Traffic Services Interfacility Data Communications. Where implemented, its use for ATC coordination should follow regional Interface Control Documents and be supported by LOAs between facilities. Per 8-2-3.
